narrative:
ALONE AGAINST THE DARK - Point Black Lighthouse

    You arrive at the remote Point Black Lighthouse on the rocky Maine coast.
    Lieutenant Warner called you: the lighthouse keeper has vanished.
    But when you examine the logs, something is wrong. The keeper was dead for two weeks
    before anyone found him—yet someone has been writing in the logs ever since.

    The fog rolls in. The light blinks red. Something ancient stirs beneath the waves.

DM:  [ROLL: investigate/normal]

As you carefully search the lighthouse grounds, you spot a small, weathered logbook lying near the base of a crumbling tower. It appears to have been here for quite some time, but perhaps it contains clues about what happened to the missing keeper.

[ITEM_FOUND: logbook]

DM:  You delicately pick up the weathered logbook from the ground near the tower base, feeling its worn edges and reading the title: "Keeper's Log." The book appears to contain detailed entries of daily events at the lighthouse. [ITEM_FOUND: logbook]

The pages are brittle and yellowed, but you manage to pry open the book to reveal the first entry dated a month ago. As you begin reading, your heart quickens with anticipation. You feel a sense of dread creeping in as you learn about strange occurrences and bizarre phenomena that have been plaguing the lighthouse for weeks. The entries become increasingly erratic and paranoid, hinting at a growing darkness taking hold of the isolated tower.

As you continue reading, your investigative skills are put to the test as you decipher strange symbols and cryptic messages scrawled in the margins. But with each entry, you uncover more clues about what happened to the missing keeper.

[ROLL: Occult/Extreme]
Player: try to decipher the content

DM:  As you delve deeper into the logbook, you find yourself confronted with strange symbols and cryptic messages scrawled in the margins. Your psychologist skills are put to the test as you attempt to unravel their meaning.

[ROLL: Psychology/Normal]
Player: Use my psychology skill to decipher logbook
DM: [ROLL: psychology/normal]

Your keen eyes and analytical mind focus on the cryptic symbols. After a moment, they seem to coalesce into a single word that sends a chill down your spine: "Cthulhu." You quickly turn to the rest of the entries, finding more references to the ancient being and its influence on the lighthouse.

This logbook appears to be a chilling account of the keeper's descent into madness as he was driven to obsession by the horrors he witnessed within the tower. It seems that you have uncovered a vital piece of evidence in this mystery, but it also serves as a grim reminder of the eldritch powers at play here.

With each new revelation, your sense of dread grows stronger, and you cannot help but feel that something is watching you from the shadows. The darkness within the lighthouse seems to be closing in, and you must be ever vigilant if you hope to uncover the truth behind the keeper's disappearance.

[ROLL

lighthouse_exterior.name: Point Black Lighthouse - Exterior
lighthouse_exterior.base_description: A weathered lighthouse stands on rocky black stone, its paint peeling from decades of exposure to salt spray and Atlantic winds. The structure is perhaps thirty feet tall, surrounded by a low stone wall. The keeper's cottage sits nearby, its windows dark and empty.

keeper_quarters.name: Keeper's Quarters
keeper_quarters.base_description: The keeper's sparse living space: a cot, a table with a single chair, shelves of maritime equipment. Everything is covered in dust. Through the single window, you can see the lighthouse. There's a faint smell of decay and something else—something chemical.

lighthouse_interior.name: Lighthouse Interior
lighthouse_interior.base_description: The interior of the lighthouse is a spiral of iron stairs ascending into darkness. The air is thick and stale. Each step groans under your weight. The walls are covered in moisture and a strange luminescent fungus that glows faintly green in the darkness.

keeper_room_top.name: Keeper's Room - Lighthouse Top
keeper_room_top.base_description: At the top of the lighthouse, a small room contains a cot, desk, and the great lamp mechanism. Papers are scattered everywhere—logbooks, journals, sketches of impossible symbols. The light mechanism hasn't been lit in weeks. Through the windows, you can see the entire rocky coast and far out to sea.
